fields.label: Discontinued
fields.tooltip_md: Define 'Discontinued' according to contract. If not provable with evidence, output 'unk' with unknown_reason.

fields.label: Price (MSRP)
fields.tooltip_md: MSRP price bracket or stated MSRP. Keep as a string token; do not guess if not public.

fields.label: Design
fields.tooltip_md: Define 'Design' according to contract. If not provable with evidence, output 'unk' with unknown_reason.

fields.label: Lighting
fields.tooltip_md: Define 'Lighting' according to contract. If not provable with evidence, output 'unk' with unknown_reason.

fields.tooltip_md: Define 'Release Date' according to contract. If not provable with evidence, output 'unk' with unknown_reason.

fields.label: RGB
fields.tooltip_md: Define 'RGB' according to contract. If not provable with evidence, output 'unk' with unknown_reason.

fields.label: Connection
fields.tooltip_md: Primary connection mode (wired/wireless/hybrid/bluetooth). Prefer manufacturer docs.

fields.label: Connectivity
fields.tooltip_md: Connectivity options (e.g., 2.4GHz, Bluetooth, USB). Use list if multiple.

fields.label: SKU
fields.tooltip_md: Define 'SKU / Part #' according to contract. If not provable with evidence, output 'unk' with unknown_reason.

aliases: Swift Pair (supporting signal only when the same source identifies the mouse)

fields.tooltip_md: Define 'Bluetooth' according to contract. If not provable with evidence, output 'unk' with unknown_reason.

fields.label: Colors
fields.tooltip_md: Colorway tokens. Use list<string>. Normalize grey?gray; multi-colorways may use '+'.

fields.label: Wireless Charging
fields.tooltip_md: Define 'Wireless Charging' according to contract. If not provable with evidence, output 'unk' with unknown_reason.

fields.label: Battery Life (Hours)
fields.tooltip_md: Define 'Battery Life (Hours)' according to contract. If not provable with evidence, output 'unk' with unknown_reason.

HyperFlex -> marketing phrase unless material is also stated
fields.label: Cable Type
fields.tooltip_md: Define 'Cable Type' according to contract. If not provable with evidence, output 'unk' with unknown_reason.

fields.tooltip_md: Define 'Paracord' according to contract. If not provable with evidence, output 'unk' with unknown_reason.

fields.tooltip_md: Define 'Computer-side Connector' according to contract. If not provable with evidence, output 'unk' with unknown_reason.

fields.label: Mouse-side Connector
fields.tooltip_md: Define 'Mouse-side Connector' according to contract. If not provable with evidence, output 'unk' with unknown_reason.

fields.tooltip_md: Define 'Coating' according to contract. If not provable with evidence, output 'unk' with unknown_reason.

fields.label: Feet Material
fields.tooltip_md: Define 'Feet Material' according to contract. If not provable with evidence, output 'unk' with unknown_reason.

fields.label: Honeycomb Frame
fields.tooltip_md: Define 'Honeycomb Frame' according to contract. If not provable with evidence, output 'unk' with unknown_reason.

fields.label: Adjustable Weight
fields.tooltip_md: Define 'Adjustable Weight' according to contract. If not provable with evidence, output 'unk' with unknown_reason.

fields.label: Weight
fields.tooltip_md: Mouse weight in grams. Convert oz?g when necessary; keep decimals if provided.

fields.label: Length
fields.tooltip_md: Overall length in millimeters. Convert inches/cm to mm; require unit when parsing.

fields.label: Width
fields.tooltip_md: Overall width in millimeters. Convert inches/cm to mm; require unit when parsing.

fields.label: Height
fields.tooltip_md: Overall height in millimeters. Convert inches/cm to mm; require unit when parsing.

fields.label: Shape
fields.tooltip_md: Define 'Shape' according to contract. If not provable with evidence, output 'unk' with unknown_reason.

fields.label: Form Factor
fields.tooltip_md: Define 'Form Factor' according to contract. If not provable with evidence, output 'unk' with unknown_reason.

fields.label: Hump
fields.tooltip_md: Define 'Hump' according to contract. If not provable with evidence, output 'unk' with unknown_reason.

fields.label: Front Flare
fields.tooltip_md: Define 'Front Flare' according to contract. If not provable with evidence, output 'unk' with unknown_reason.

fields.label: Thumb Rest
fields.tooltip_md: Define 'Thumb Rest' according to contract. If not provable with evidence, output 'unk' with unknown_reason.

aliases: all grip types -> palm + claw + fingertip
universal grip support -> palm + claw + fingertip
fields.label: Grip
fields.tooltip_md: Define 'Grip' according to contract. If not provable with evidence, output 'unk' with unknown_reason.

fields.label: Hand Size
fields.tooltip_md: Define 'Hand Size' according to contract. If not provable with evidence, output 'unk' with unknown_reason.

aliases: Razer Focus Pro 35K Optical Sensor Gen-2
Razer Focus Pro 45K Optical Sensor Gen-2
Razer Focus Pro 50K Optical Sensor Gen-3
fields.label: Sensor
fields.tooltip_md: Sensor model (component DB-backed). Must be evidence-backed; avoid wrong-variant merges.

fields.label: Sensor Brand
fields.tooltip_md: Sensor manufacturer brand (e.g., PixArt). Prefer component DB if sensor identified.

aliases: IR LED/infrared LED/LED illumination -> optical when tied to the motion sensor
fields.label: Sensor Type
fields.tooltip_md: Define 'Sensor Type' according to contract. If not provable with evidence, output 'unk' with unknown_reason.

fields.guidance_md: Extract the sensor component announcement, launch, first-public availability, or first-supported date, not the ordinary mouse release date. Use product launch/source dates only when the cited text explicitly introduces, debuts, reveals, or first-supports the sensor. Normalize exact dates as YYYY-MM-DD; month-only precision may use YYYY-MM-01 with evidence explaining the precision. Treat sensor_date <= release_date as a soft sanity check only, because firmware upgrades, rebadges, or later tuned generations can post-date an original mouse release.
fields.label: Sensor Date
fields.tooltip_md: Sensor component announcement, launch, first-public availability, or first-supported date. Do not enforce sensor_date <= release_date as a hard rule.

fields.guidance_md: Store the exact sensor component source URL: manufacturer component page, datasheet/spec PDF, official support/technology page naming the exact sensor, or authorized component distributor fallback. Product pages, reviews, consumer marketplaces, sensor databases, forums, and articles that merely mention the sensor are not valid sensor_link values.
fields.label: Sensor Link
fields.tooltip_md: Exact sensor component URL only. Use unknown when no component-level maker/distributor source is proven.

fields.guidance_md: Boolean yes/no only. Use true for exact-product or resolved-sensor evidence of 1:1/raw/flawless tracking, or zero/no smoothing, filtering, acceleration, prediction, angle snapping, or drift correction. Use false only for credible non-disableable tracking flaws. Omission or absence from curated lists is unknown, not false.
fields.label: Flawless Sensor
fields.tooltip_md: Define 'Flawless Sensor' according to contract. If not provable with evidence, output 'unk' with unknown_reason.

fields.guidance_md: Extract the maximum effective mouse sensor resolution/sensitivity for the exact product as integer DPI/CPI. CPI and DPI are equivalent here. Use the highest value from a proven range or fixed settings list. Reject default/current DPI stages, player/eDPI settings, in-game sensitivity, polling/report rate, IPS, acceleration, and sibling sensor-generation values.
fields.label: Max DPI
fields.tooltip_md: Maximum DPI/CPI as an integer for the exact product or exact resolved sensor version.

fields.guidance_md: Extract maximum sensor tracking speed as numeric IPS for the exact product or exact resolved sensor. Max speed/tracking speed/inches per second can qualify when tied to sensor motion tracking. Do not use scroll speed, DPI/CPI, polling rate, acceleration, or sibling comparison-table values.
fields.label: Max IPS
fields.tooltip_md: Define 'Max IPS' according to contract. If not provable with evidence, output 'unk' with unknown_reason.

fields.guidance_md: Extract maximum rated sensor tracking acceleration in G as a number. Do not convert zero/no acceleration processing claims to 0, and do not confuse grams of weight with acceleration.
fields.label: Max Acceleration
fields.tooltip_md: Define 'Max Acceleration' according to contract. If not provable with evidence, output 'unk' with unknown_reason.

fields.guidance_md: Extract supported polling/report/USB report rates in Hz for the exact SKU and receiver bundle. Return explicitly selectable rates; if only an up-to/maximum rate is proven, return only that maximum. Convert response-time shorthand such as 1 ms to 1000 Hz and 0.125 ms to 8000 Hz. Do not union across variants unless the requested identity is a family-level listing.
fields.label: Polling Rate
fields.tooltip_md: Polling/report rate options in Hz. Normalize to a deduped descending list; do not invent lower steps from a maximum-only claim.

fields.label: Lift-off Distance (LOD)
fields.tooltip_md: Lift-off distance (LOD) in mm. May be a range or set; normalize ranges to {min,max}.

fields.label: Lift Settings
fields.tooltip_md: Define 'Lift Settings' according to contract. If not provable with evidence, output 'unk' with unknown_reason.

fields.guidance_md:
Sensor Latency is a single numeric ms field, not a comma-delimited list.
Keep mode-specific measurements in sensor_latency_wired, sensor_latency_wireless, sensor_latency_bluetooth.
Only fill this scalar when a single authoritative value is reported or the source clearly names the primary mode value; otherwise output unk.
fields.label: Sensor Latency
fields.tooltip_md: Sensor Latency as one numeric ms value. Mode-specific values belong in the separate wired/wireless/bluetooth fields.

fields.label: Sensor Latency (Wired)
fields.tooltip_md: Define 'Sensor Latency' according to contract. If not provable with evidence, output 'unk' with unknown_reason.

fields.label: Sensor Latency (Wireless)
fields.tooltip_md: Define 'Sensor Latency' according to contract. If not provable with evidence, output 'unk' with unknown_reason.

fields.label: Sensor Latency (Bluetooth)
fields.tooltip_md: Define 'Sensor Latency' according to contract. If not provable with evidence, output 'unk' with unknown_reason.

fields.label: DPI Shift Latency
fields.tooltip_md: Define 'DPI Shift Latency' according to contract. If not provable with evidence, output 'unk' with unknown_reason.

aliases: synchronizes mouse reports with PC polling intervals
synchronizes sensor data with USB polling events
synchronizes USB polling events with sensor data
syncs the mouse signals to your PC intervals
fields.label: Motion Sync
fields.tooltip_md: Define 'Motion Sync' according to contract. If not provable with evidence, output 'unk' with unknown_reason.

fields.label: Hardware Acceleration
fields.tooltip_md: Define 'Hardware Acceleration' according to contract. If not provable with evidence, output 'unk' with unknown_reason.

fields.label: Smoothing
fields.tooltip_md: Define 'Smoothing' according to contract. If not provable with evidence, output 'unk' with unknown_reason.

aliases: Officially verified for NVIDIA Reflex Latency Analyzer
fields.label: NVIDIA Reflex
fields.tooltip_md: Define 'NVIDIA Reflex' according to contract. If not provable with evidence, output 'unk' with unknown_reason.

fields.guidance_md: Extract the stock primary left/right click switch identity. Do not use scroll encoder, side-button switches, switch_type, hot-swap support, or click lifespan. Brand-only or technology-only wording belongs to switch_brand/switch_type unless marketed as the switch component name.
fields.label: Switch
fields.tooltip_md: Stock primary click switch model/name, component DB-backed. Exact product evidence required.

fields.guidance_md: Extract the primary left/right click switch brand as shipped on the exact SKU. Normalize exact switch models to the canonical maker/proprietary brand list. Do not infer from mouse brand, generic optical/mechanical wording, click rating, quiet clicks, or optional replacement switch packs.
fields.label: Switch Brand
fields.tooltip_md: Primary click switch brand/maker. Exact switch model belongs in switch.

fields.guidance_md: Extract only factory/default left/right main click switch technology. Keep to inductive, mechanical, optical, or optical / mechanical. Do not use optical sensor type, encoder technology, hot-swap compatibility, quiet-click marketing, optional switch packs, model names, or lifecycle claims as chips.
fields.label: Switch Type
fields.tooltip_md: Main click switch technology: inductive, mechanical, optical, or optical / mechanical.

fields.guidance_md: Return the exact switch component URL, not a mouse product/review/spec page. Valid targets are maker component pages, maker datasheets/spec PDFs, official exact proprietary switch-technology pages, or authorized component distributor pages for the exact part. Return unknown if the exact switch identity or component URL is not proven.
fields.label: Switch Link
fields.tooltip_md: Exact switch component URL only. Product pages and reviews are not valid switch_link targets.

fields.guidance_md: Boolean yes/no for mouse button micro-switch sockets. True requires exact-mouse evidence for hot-swappable/user-replaceable/push-fit/no-solder click switches. Do not count keyboard switches, batteries, shells, side plates, dongles, mouse feet, or generic spare switches.
fields.label: Hot Swappable
fields.tooltip_md: Hot-swappable mouse button micro-switch sockets. Absence is unknown, not false.

fields.guidance_md: Extract left/right main-button click or actuation force in gf. Prefer exact product measurements/specs; otherwise use the proven switch component nominal operating force. Do not use release force, lifespan/click count, latency/debounce, actuation distance, pre/post-travel, or L/R deviation.
fields.label: Click Force
fields.tooltip_md: Main-button click/actuation force in gf. Product-level measurement can override switch nominal force.

fields.guidance_md: Click Latency is one numeric ms value for average main-button click-to-computer/report latency. Prefer lab measurements. Use the overall/expected/primary connection value when the source provides one; otherwise choose wired for wired-only, receiver for wireless gaming, or Bluetooth for Bluetooth-only/productivity-first products. Do not use sensor latency, polling interval, debounce, switch actuation, click force, or relative/baseline-adjusted values.
fields.label: Click Latency
fields.tooltip_md: Average main-button click-to-report latency in ms. Mode-specific values belong in the wired/wireless/Bluetooth fields.

fields.guidance_md: Extract measured main-button click/button latency in wired USB input mode only. Do not use receiver, Bluetooth, expected connection, polling interval, HyperPolling response-time marketing, sensor latency, debounce, shift latency, click force, or MouseSpecs offset rows.
fields.label: Click Latency (Wired)
fields.tooltip_md: Wired USB click/button latency in ms.

fields.guidance_md: Extract non-Bluetooth receiver/dongle main-click latency only. Do not use wired rows, Bluetooth rows, polling/report-rate math, optical switch response time, debounce, sensor/motion latency, display/system input lag, or MouseSpecs comparison offsets.
fields.label: Click Latency (Wireless)
fields.tooltip_md: Receiver/proprietary wireless main-click latency in ms.

fields.guidance_md: Extract average main-button click-to-registration latency measured in Bluetooth mode only. Reject overall/expected, wired, receiver, sensor latency, polling rate, debounce, actuation, wake-up latency, and generic 0 ms/1 ms marketing.
fields.label: Click Latency (Bluetooth)
fields.tooltip_md: Bluetooth-mode main-click latency in ms. Unknown if no exact Bluetooth row/value exists.

fields.guidance_md: Extract firmware/software click debounce delay settings in ms. Return all discrete options; for a numeric range, return min and max endpoints only. Normalize explicit zero/no debounce to 0 ms. Do not use measured click latency, actuation time, OS double-click speed, multiclick filters, slamclick/SPDT/GX modes, or unmapped Fast/Normal labels.
fields.label: Debounce
fields.tooltip_md: Click debounce delay setting in ms. Numeric settings only.

fields.guidance_md: Boolean yes/no for intentionally quiet/silent click support. True requires exact-product silent/quiet/noiseless/mute click wording. False requires explicit negative evidence; absence is unknown.
fields.label: Silent Clicks
fields.tooltip_md: Silent/quiet click feature. Do not infer false from silence.

fields.guidance_md: Count remappable mouse inputs only when a source gives a programmable/remappable/configurable count. Do not infer from visible buttons, side-button count, or total physical button count alone.
fields.label: Programmable Buttons
fields.tooltip_md: Number of programmable/remappable mouse inputs. Physical button count alone is not enough.

fields.guidance_md: Count physical side-mounted button actuators only. Use clear side/360/PIF images when specs are absent. Exclude primary clicks, wheel inputs, top/bottom buttons, side scroll wheels, lighting zones, and total programmable-button counts.
fields.label: Side Buttons
fields.tooltip_md: Physical side-mounted button count. Visual/PIF evidence can support counting.

fields.guidance_md: Count only extra dedicated top-center buttons near the scroll wheel. Use official layout/manual, clear top-view image, or normalized source. Exclude scroll-wheel click, wheel tilt, side/thumb buttons, underside controls, and total/programmable button counts.
fields.label: Middle Buttons
fields.tooltip_md: Extra top-center button count. Visual/PIF top views can support counting.

fields.guidance_md: Boolean yes/no for main scroll-wheel left/right tilt-click inputs. Do not count separate thumb/side wheels, horizontal scrolling without wheel tilt, free-spin/smart-shift mode, body tilt, or software-only horizontal scrolling.
fields.label: Tilt Scroll Wheel
fields.tooltip_md: True only when the main scroll wheel tilts left/right.

fields.guidance_md: Boolean yes/no for user-adjustable or mode-switchable scroll-wheel feel/behavior. True covers ratchet/free-spin modes, configurable scroll tension/resistance/steps, Smart-Reel, MagSpeed, HyperScroll, or HyperScroll Pro. Do not count tilt/horizontal scrolling, OS/app scroll speed, scroll acceleration alone, or fixed step-count wheels.
fields.label: Adjustable Scroll Wheel
fields.tooltip_md: Adjustable or mode-switchable scroll-wheel behavior. Absence of marketing text is unknown, not false.

fields.guidance_md: Boolean yes/no only. Treat onboard memory/profile-storage phrases as aliases for extraction, not public enum chips. Do not infer false from silence.
fields.label: Onboard Memory
fields.tooltip_md: Define 'Onboard Memory' according to contract. If not provable with evidence, output 'unk' with unknown_reason.

fields.guidance_md: Not an enum. Store only the integer count of onboard memory/profile slots. Use unk when onboard memory is known but no count is sourced.
fields.label: Onboard Memory (Value)
fields.tooltip_md: Define 'Onboard Memory (Value)' according to contract. If not provable with evidence, output 'unk' with unknown_reason.

fields.guidance_md: Boolean yes/no only. Profile switching requires a user-visible profile switch/cycle/select action; DPI/CPI switching and software-only app profile switching do not count.
fields.label: Profile Switching
fields.tooltip_md: Define 'Profile Switching' according to contract. If not provable with evidence, output 'unk' with unknown_reason.

fields.label: Editions
fields.tooltip_md: Special/limited editions. Kebab-case slugs (e.g. cyberpunk-2077-edition).

fields.guidance_md: Extract the scroll-wheel encoder component identity only. Component link fields must target exact component maker pages, datasheets/spec PDFs, maker docs, or authorized component distributor pages, not product pages/reviews/consumer marketplaces.
fields.label: Encoder
fields.tooltip_md: Define 'Encoder' according to contract. If not provable with evidence, output 'unk' with unknown_reason.

fields.guidance_md: Store only the canonical encoder maker/brand. Exact encoder model/color/grade/height/step/type/lifespan belongs in encoder or component properties, not this chip.
fields.label: Encoder Brand
fields.tooltip_md: Define 'Encoder Brand' according to contract. If not provable with evidence, output 'unk' with unknown_reason.

fields.label: Encoder Link
fields.tooltip_md: Define 'Encoder Link' according to contract. If not provable with evidence, output 'unk' with unknown_reason.

fields.guidance_md: Extract the exact mouse-body MCU/SoC part only. Do not store receiver, dongle, charging dock, cable-side USB controller, generic MCU family/core wording, or inferred sibling values. Component link hygiene belongs on mcu_link.
fields.label: MCU
fields.tooltip_md: Define 'MCU' according to contract. If not provable with evidence, output 'unk' with unknown_reason.

fields.guidance_md: Submit only an exact MCU/SoC component-level URL: maker product page, datasheet/spec PDF, official documentation page, or authorized distributor part page. Product pages, reviews, forums, marketplaces, and spec aggregators can support identity but are not valid mcu_link targets.
fields.label: MCU Link
fields.tooltip_md: Define 'MCU Link' according to contract. If not provable with evidence, output 'unk' with unknown_reason.
